Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: 1. Analyze the Request: Input: A pseudo-BPMN representation of a customer request handling process (Standard vs. Custom paths, validation, checks, approval, invoicing, confirmation). Task: Optimize the process to reduce turnaround time, increase flexibility for non-standard requests, leverage automation, dynamic resource allocation, and predictive analytics. Requirements: Discuss changes to tasks, propose new gateways/subprocesses, explain impacts on performance, customer satisfaction, and operational complexity. Format: Complex Open-Ended Question response (needs to be structured, analytical, and comprehensive).

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 2. Deconstruct the Existing Process (Pain Points Identification): Sequential Bottlenecks: Receive Request -> Check Type -> Validation -> Parallel Checks -> Date Calc -> Approval. This is linear. Late Decision Points: Approval happens after validation and date calculation. If rejected, re-evaluation loops back, wasting time. Manual Intervention: Obtain Manager Approval and Re-evaluate Conditions imply human bottlenecks. Rigid Routing: The split happens immediately at Check Request Type. If a request is borderline, it might be misrouted. Reactive: Customization feasibility is checked after the request is received and analyzed. No proactive identification.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: 3. Brainstorming Optimization Strategies: Automation: RPA for data entry, auto-validation, auto-invoicing for standard cases. Predictive Analytics: Classify requests before they enter the workflow based on historical data (e.g., NLP on request text). Dynamic Resources: Pooling resources for Custom Feasibility instead of fixed queues. Parallelism: Move approval earlier or make it conditional/automated based on risk scores. Flexibility: Allow hybrid paths (Standard + Minor Customization) without full Custom path overhead.

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 4. Drafting the Redesign (Iterative Process): Phase 1: Ingestion & Classification (The Brain). Instead of a simple XOR gateway at the start, use an AI-driven classifier. Change: Replace Check Request Type with AI Request Classification & Risk Scoring. Benefit: Proactive routing. Phase 2: Validation & Checks (The Hands). Change: Automate Credit and Inventory checks (APIs). Merge Standard Validation with Parallel Checks. New Concept: Dynamic Resource Pooling for Custom Feasibility. Phase 3: Approval (The Bottleneck). Change: Implement Automated Approval Thresholds. Only high-risk/high-value go to humans. New Gateway: Risk Threshold Check before Manager Approval. Phase 4: Feedback Loop. Change: Continuous learning from Re-evaluation to improve the AI classifier.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: 5. Structuring the Response: Executive Summary: High-level overview of the optimization philosophy (Shift from Linear to Intelligent/Adaptive). Detailed Redesign Proposal: Break down by process stage (Ingestion, Execution, Decision, Closure). New Pseudo-BPMN Snippet: Visualize the key changes (text-based representation). Impact Analysis: Address Performance, Customer Satisfaction, and Operational Complexity as requested. Implementation Considerations: Risks and prerequisites (Data, Tech Stack).

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: 6. Refining the Content (Self-Correction/Polishing): Critique: Did I address dynamic reallocation of resources? Fix: Explicitly mention a Skill-Based Routing Engine for the Custom Feasibility task. Critique: Did I address predictive analytics? Fix: Ensure the initial classification uses ML models to predict customization likelihood. Critique: Is the loop back handled better? Fix: Instead of looping back to E1/D, use a Revision Subprocess that tracks versioning to avoid confusion. Tone: Professional, consultative, strategic.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: 7. Final Review against Constraints: Did I discuss changes to tasks? Yes. Did I propose new gateways/subprocesses? Yes. Did I explain impacts on performance, satisfaction, complexity? Yes.
